summary: Machine policy for limiting diagnostic dump collection. The local Microsoft DataCollection.admx file explicitly documents the same DataCollection policy key and LimitDumpCollection value name that the app uses, and the ADML help text explains the effect.

setting.name: Diagnostic Dump Collection
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can collect diagnostic dump data for troubleshooting. This policy decides whether that dump collection is limited.
setting.why_people_change_it: Privacy-focused or tightly governed environments may want to reduce how much dump data can be collected from the device.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Limiting dump collection reduces potentially sensitive diagnostic data, but it can also weaken troubleshooting quality when crashes or failures need deeper analysis.
setting.professional_notes: DataCollection.admx maps this machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\LimitDumpCollection with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local crash-dump source mirror shows CrashDumpEnabled and AllowCrashDump as separate CrashControl values, so this policy should be treated as dump-limiting behavior rather than crash-dump enablement.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force this dump-collection limitation policy.

allowed_values.label: Do not limit dump collection
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and dump collection is not restricted by this setting.

allowed_values.label: Limit dump collection
allowed_values.meaning: Diagnostic dump collection is limited by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes LimitDumpCollection = 1, which matches the documented limit behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 2004 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This describes only the policy baseline and does not replace crash-dump specific configuration analysis.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that may need the fullest diagnostic dump evidence
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal policy baseline for dump collection.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to reduce diagnostic dump data collection.
tradeoffs: Dump collection is not restricted by this policy.
More diagnostic evidence can remain available for troubleshooting.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
intended_for: Managed environments with stricter data-minimization goals
avoid_for: Scenarios that need strong post-crash diagnostic evidence

states.rationale: Limit diagnostic dump collection by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to reduce how much dump data can be collected from the device.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need richer diagnostic dumps for support or engineering analysis.
tradeoffs: Potentially sensitive dump data is reduced.
Crash and failure analysis may have less evidence to work with.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: LimitDumpCollection
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that limits diagnostic dump collection.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows LimitDumpCollection with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the DataCollection policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes LimitDumpCollection = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto crash-dump gate trace
evidence.summary: IopInitializeDumpPolicySettings.c reads AllowCrashDump and IopCrashDumpPolicyChangeWnfCallback uses it to configure crash dumping, while crashdmp.c shows CrashDumpEnabled in the CrashControl block; these are separate from LimitDumpCollection.

decision.why: The local Microsoft DataCollection.admx file explicitly defines LimitDumpCollection under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection with valueName LimitDumpCollection, and the paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy limits Windows Error Reporting to kernel mini dumps and user mode triage dumps while disabled or not configured may allow fuller dumps. The app writes that documented limiting value.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same DataCollection policy key and LimitDumpCollection value used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy limits Windows Error Reporting to kernel mini dumps and user mode triage dumps.
